heading: Other patient protections
label: 9822 Other patient protections
notes: Effective DateSection applicable with respect to plan years beginning on or after , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2020 Amendment note under , Government Organization and Employees.Jan. 1, 2022section 102(e) of div. BB of Pub. L. 116–260section 8902 of Title 5
amendments: Effective DateSection applicable with respect to plan years beginning on or after , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2020 Amendment note under , Government Organization and Employees.Jan. 1, 2022section 102(e) of div. BB of Pub. L. 116–260section 8902 of Title 5

children.heading: Choice of health care professional
children.label: a Choice of health care professional
children.text: If a group health plan requires or provides for designation by a participant or beneficiary of a participating primary care provider, then the plan shall permit each participant and beneficiary to designate any participating primary care provider who is available to accept such individual.

children.heading: Pediatric care
children.label: 1 Pediatric care
children.text: In the case of a person who has a child who is a participant or beneficiary under a group health plan if the plan requires or provides for the designation of a participating primary care provider for the child, the plan shall permit such person to designate a physician (allopathic or osteopathic) who specializes in pediatrics as the child’s primary care provider if such provider participates in the network of the plan.

children.heading: Construction
children.label: 2 Construction
children.text: Nothing in paragraph (1) shall be construed to waive any exclusions of coverage under the terms and conditions of the plan with respect to coverage of pediatric care.

children.heading: Patient access to obstetrical and gynecological care
children.label: c Patient access to obstetrical and gynecological care

children.heading: Direct access
children.label: A Direct access
children.text: A group health plan described in paragraph (2) may not require authorization or referral by the plan, issuer, or any person (including a primary care provider described in paragraph (2)(B)) in the case of a female participant or beneficiary who seeks coverage for obstetrical or gynecological care provided by a participating health care professional who specializes in obstetrics or gynecology. Such professional shall agree to otherwise adhere to such plan’s policies and procedures, including procedures regarding referrals and obtaining prior authorization and providing services pursuant to a treatment plan (if any) approved by the plan.

children.heading: Obstetrical and gynecological care
children.label: B Obstetrical and gynecological care
children.text: A group health plan described in paragraph (2) shall treat the provision of obstetrical and gynecological care, and the ordering of related obstetrical and gynecological items and services, pursuant to the direct access described under subparagraph (A), by a participating health care professional who specializes in obstetrics or gynecology as the authorization of the primary care provider.

children.label: A
children.text: provides coverage for obstetric or gynecologic care; and

children.label: B
children.text: requires the designation by a participant or beneficiary of a participating primary care provider.

children.label: A
children.text: waive any exclusions of coverage under the terms and conditions of the plan with respect to coverage of obstetrical or gynecological care; or

children.label: B
children.text: preclude the group health plan involved from requiring that the obstetrical or gynecological provider notify the primary care health care professional or the plan or issuer of treatment decisions.
